title: Feedback Evaluation Reliability and RCA Contract
description:
## Summary
Make feedback-backed evaluations operationally reliable, with root-cause analysis treated as required process output rather than best-effort optional output.

## Scope
- RCA reliability is a separate workstream from deterministic dataset design.
- The goal is a single correct RCA path for feedback-backed evaluations.
- Successful completion should mean metrics and RCA are both present when RCA is enabled.

## Key assumptions
- RCA reliability is a dependency for high-trust optimization workflows.
- Missing RCA with incorrect items is a process-critical defect.
- Evaluation orchestration should treat the evaluation record as source of truth, not local CLI exit.

## Notes
Create the child bug/tasks in this epic exactly as planned so operational reliability work stays visible and auditable.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Investigating RCA design scope: comparing current feedback-evaluation RCA flow with the reusable feedback-contradictions analysis path to design a broader malfunction-classification layer. Goal is to distinguish score-config root causes from information gaps, SME/rubric questions, and mechanical/system failures without adding fallback paths.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
## Investigation findings: RCA scope is too narrow

Current feedback evaluation RCA assumes every wrong prediction should end in a score-configuration fix. That assumption is baked into the per-exemplar helper and the topic synthesis prompts.

### Current code findings
- `FeedbackEvaluation.run()` persists `parameters.root_cause` only when the RCA result has non-empty `topics`, and the evaluation still completes if RCA is absent. See `plexus/Evaluation.py` around the feedback RCA call and persistence gate.
- `_run_root_cause_analysis()` clusters incorrect items and then asks for `suggested_fix` / `overall_improvement_suggestion`, always framed as score-configuration changes.
- The reusable helper `plexus.rca_analysis.analyze_score_result()` is also explicitly score-fix oriented: it returns `(detailed_cause, suggested_fix)` and asks for one concrete score-code change.
- Separately, `plexus.reports.blocks.feedback_contradictions.FeedbackContradictions` already contains a distinct per-item classifier for `contradiction` vs `policy_gap`, plus topic clustering and confidence/voting data.

### Design implication
RCA should not be the first or only analysis layer for misclassifications. We need a preceding or enclosing `malfunction classification` layer that classifies each wrong item into broad buckets before root-cause synthesis:
- `score_configuration_gap`
- `guideline_contradiction`
- `policy_gap_question_for_sme`
- `information_gap`
- `mechanical_system_failure`
- optionally `mixed_or_uncertain`

### Why this matters
Only the `score_configuration_gap` bucket should flow into the existing "suggest a code/config fix" RCA path. The other buckets need different next actions:
- contradiction -> invalidate/review feedback label or reconcile rubric drift
- policy gap -> SME/rubric clarification
- information gap -> acknowledge unverifiable item; do not optimize score against it
- mechanical/system_failure -> bug investigation, not prompt iteration

### Reuse / DRY direction
Do not duplicate the contradictions logic inside evaluation RCA. Extract a shared analysis module from `FeedbackContradictions` so evaluation RCA and reports can call the same per-item policy classifier and topicing primitives.

### Payload / UI direction
The evaluation payload should grow beyond `root_cause` to include a structured malfunction summary that the dashboard can surface as a glanceable breakdown by bucket and recommended next action.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Progress context: RCA contract now enforced + unified across feedback-backed paths, and small-set (<5) RCA summarization implemented to avoid empty/no-topics outcomes. For cold restarts, use plx-9aa370 runbook note for canonical evaluation execution + verification path.
